<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// 数据模型定义
// ============================================

class TestObserver : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    TestObserver(const std::string& name) : m_name(name), m_changed(false) {}

    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_changed = changed;
        m_callCount++;
        // Call base class to propagate notification to observers
        ObserverNode::valueChanged(changed);
    }

    bool wasChanged() const { return m_changed; }
    int getCallCount() const { return m_callCount; }
    const std::string& getName() const { return m_name; }

private:
    std::string m_name;
    bool m_changed;
    int m_callCount = 0;
};

// ============================================
// 函数实现
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Create observer relationship and test notification
 *
 * @param observerName Name for the observer node
 * @param targetName Name for the target node
 * @return std::pair<bool, int> Pair of whether observer was notified and call count
 */
std::pair<bool, int> test_observer_notification(const std::string& observerName, const std::string& targetName) {
    // Reset thread safety for testing
    reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance().resetForTesting();
    
    // Create observer and target nodes
    auto observer = std::make_shared<TestObserver>(observerName);
    auto target = std::make_shared<TestObserver>(targetName);
    
    // Get observer graph instance
    auto& observerGraph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // Add observer relationship (observer observes target)
    observerGraph.addObserver(observer, target);
    
    // Notify target of change
    target->valueChanged(true);
    
    // Return notification status
    return {observer->wasChanged(), observer->getCallCount()};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// 函数实现
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test atomic operations on reactive variables
 *
 * @param initialValue Initial value for the reactive variable
 * @param addValue Value to add atomically
 * @return int The final value after atomic addition
 */
int test_atomic_operations(int initialValue, int addValue) {
    // Create a reactive variable using reaction::var with the initial value
    auto reactiveVar = reaction::var(initialValue);
    
    // Use the += operator to perform atomic addition
    reactiveVar += addValue;
    
    // Use the get() method to retrieve the current value
    // and return the result
    return reactiveVar.get();
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>
#include <vector>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Data model definitions
// ============================================

class TestMultipleObserver : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    TestMultipleObserver(const std::string& name) : m_name(name), m_changed(false) {}

    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_changed = changed;
        m_callCount++;
    }

    bool wasChanged() const { return m_changed; }
    int getCallCount() const { return m_callCount; }
    const std::string& getName() const { return m_name; }

    void reset() {
        m_changed = false;
        m_callCount = 0;
    }

private:
    std::string m_name;
    bool m_changed;
    int m_callCount = 0;
};

// ============================================
// Function implementations
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test multiple observers watching a single target
 *
 * @param targetName Name for the target node
 * @param observerNames Vector of names for observer nodes
 * @return std::pair<int, bool> Pair of total call count and whether all observers were notified
 */
std::pair<int, bool> test_multiple_observers(const std::string& targetName, const std::vector<std::string>& observerNames) {
    // 1. Create target node
    auto target = std::make_shared<TestMultipleObserver>(targetName);
    
    // 2. Create observer nodes
    std::vector<std::shared_ptr<TestMultipleObserver>> observers;
    for (const auto& name : observerNames) {
        observers.push_back(std::make_shared<TestMultipleObserver>(name));
    }
    
    // 3. Get observer graph instance
    auto& graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // 4. Add all observers to watch target
    for (auto& observer : observers) {
        graph.addObserver(target, observer);
    }
    
    // 5. Notify target
    target->valueChanged(true);
    
    // 6. Count notifications and verify all observers were notified
    int totalCallCount = 0;
    bool allNotified = true;
    
    for (auto& observer : observers) {
        totalCallCount += observer->getCallCount();
        if (observer->getCallCount() == 0) {
            allNotified = false;
        }
    }
    
    // 7. Return total call count and notification status
    return {totalCallCount, allNotified};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Data model definitions
// ============================================

class TestChainedObserver : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    TestChainedObserver(const std::string& name) : m_name(name), m_changed(false) {}

    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_changed = changed;
        m_callCount++;
        // Call base class to propagate notification to observers
        ObserverNode::valueChanged(changed);
    }

    bool wasChanged() const { return m_changed; }
    int getCallCount() const { return m_callCount; }
    const std::string& getName() const { return m_name; }

    void reset() {
        m_changed = false;
        m_callCount = 0;
    }

private:
    std::string m_name;
    bool m_changed;
    int m_callCount = 0;
};

// ============================================
// Function implementations
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test chained observer relationships
 *
 * @param targetName Name for the target node
 * @param observer1Name Name for the first observer node
 * @param observer2Name Name for the second observer node (observes observer1)
 * @return std::pair<int, bool> Pair of total call count and whether chain notification worked correctly
 */
std::pair<int, bool> test_chained_observers(const std::string& targetName, 
                                            const std::string& observer1Name, 
                                            const std::string& observer2Name) {
    // 1. Create target node
    auto target = std::make_shared<TestChainedObserver>(targetName);
    
    // 2. Create first observer node
    auto observer1 = std::make_shared<TestChainedObserver>(observer1Name);
    
    // 3. Create second observer node
    auto observer2 = std::make_shared<TestChainedObserver>(observer2Name);
    
    // 4. Get observer graph instance
    auto& graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // 5. Add target as observer to observer1 (observer1 observes target)
    graph.addObserver(observer1, target);
    
    // 6. Add observer1 as observer to observer2 (observer2 observes observer1)
    graph.addObserver(observer2, observer1);
    
    // 7. Reset all change flags
    target->reset();
    observer1->reset();
    observer2->reset();
    
    // 8. Notify target
    target->valueChanged(true);
    
    // 9. Check if observer1 was notified
    bool observer1Notified = observer1->wasChanged();
    
    // 10. Check if observer2 was notified  
    bool observer2Notified = observer2->wasChanged();
    
    // 11. Calculate total call count
    int totalCalls = observer1->getCallCount() + observer2->getCallCount();
    
    // 12. Chain status: both observers must have been notified
    bool chainStatus = observer1Notified && observer2Notified;
    
    // 13. Return pair with total calls and chain status
    return std::make_pair(totalCalls, chainStatus);
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Function implementations
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test reactive action with side effects
 *
 * @param initialValue Initial value for the reactive variable
 * @param newValue New value to set
 * @return int The value captured by the action (should be newValue after update)
 */
int test_reactive_action(int initialValue, int newValue) {
    // 1. Create reactive variable
    auto reactiveVar = reaction::var(initialValue);
    
    // 2. Create variable to capture side effects
    int capturedValue = 0;
    
    // 3. Create reactive action
    auto action = reaction::action([&]() {
        // 4. Update reactive variable
        reactiveVar.value(newValue);
        
        // Capture the current value
        capturedValue = reactiveVar.get();
    });
    
    // Execute the action
    action();
    
    // 5. Return captured value
    return capturedValue;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Function implementations
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test resetting a calculation node with new dependencies
 *
 * @param a First input value
 * @param b Second input value
 * @param c Third input value
 * @return int The result after resetting calculation to use different dependencies
 */
int test_calculation_reset(int a, int b, int c) {
    // Create reactive variables
    auto var_a = reaction::var(a);
    auto var_b = reaction::var(b);
    auto var_c = reaction::var(c);
    
    // Create initial calculation: a + b
    auto calc_node = reaction::calc([](int x, int y) { return x + y; }, var_a, var_b);
    
    // Reset calculation to use different dependencies: b + c
    calc_node.reset([](int x, int y) { return x + y; }, var_b, var_c);
    
    // Return result after reset
    return calc_node.get();
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Data model definitions
// ============================================

class PersonRecord : public reaction::FieldBase {
public:
    PersonRecord(const std::string& name, int age)
        : m_name(name), m_age(age) {}

    const std::string& getName() const { return m_name.get(); }
    int getAge() const { return m_age.get(); }

    void setName(const std::string& name) { m_name.value(name); }
    void setAge(int age) { m_age.value(age); }

private:
    reaction::Var<std::string> m_name;
    reaction::Var<int> m_age;
};

// ============================================
// Function implementations
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test reactive fields updating calculated info
 *
 * @param initialName Initial person name
 * @param initialAge Initial person age
 * @param newName Updated person name
 * @param newAge Updated person age
 * @return std::string Concatenated info after updates
 */
std::string test_reactive_fields(const std::string& initialName,
                                 int initialAge,
                                 const std::string& newName,
                                 int newAge) {
    // 1. Create reactive PersonRecord variable
    auto person = reaction::var(PersonRecord(initialName, initialAge));
    
    // 2. Create calculation depending on reactive fields
    auto calcInfo = reaction::calc([&person]() {
        return "Name: " + person.get().getName() + ", Age: " + std::to_string(person.get().getAge());
    });
    
    // 3. Update name field
    person.get().setName(newName);
    
    // 4. Update age field
    person.get().setAge(newAge);
    
    // 5. Return updated info
    return calcInfo();
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <tuple>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Function implementations
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test batch execution reduces recomputation count
 *
 * @param initialA Initial value for variable a
 * @param initialB Initial value for variable b
 * @param nonBatchA Value to set a without batching
 * @param nonBatchB Value to set b without batching
 * @param batchA Value to set a within batch
 * @param batchB Value to set b within batch
 * @return std::tuple<int, int, int> {final sum, count after non-batch updates, count after batch updates}
 */
std::tuple<int, int, int> test_batch_execution(int initialA,
                                               int initialB,
                                               int nonBatchA,
                                               int nonBatchB,
                                               int batchA,
                                               int batchB) {
    using namespace reaction;
    
    // 1. Create reactive variables
    auto a = var(initialA);
    auto b = var(initialB);
    
    // 2. Create variable to track recomputation count
    auto recompCount = var(0);
    
    // 3. Create calculation that sums a and b and tracks recomputation count
    auto sumCalc = calc([&]() {
        recompCount.value(recompCount.get() + 1);
        return a.get() + b.get();
    });
    
    // Force initial computation to establish baseline
    int initialSum = sumCalc.get();
    
    // 4. Perform non-batch updates
    a.value(nonBatchA);
    b.value(nonBatchB);
    
    // 5. Record recompute count after non-batch updates
    int nonBatchCount = recompCount.get();
    
    // 6. Create batch context and 7. Execute batch
    {
        Batch batch([&]() {
            a.value(batchA);
            b.value(batchB);
        });
        // Batch automatically executes when Batch object is created
    }
    
    // 8. Record recompute count after batch updates
    int batchCount = recompCount.get();
    
    // 9. Return final sum and counts
    int finalSum = sumCalc.get();
    
    return {finalSum, nonBatchCount, batchCount};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<think>We are to implement the function `test_named_reactions` using the `reaction` library.
 The function should:
  1. Create reactive variables with names (using `reaction::var` and `setName`)
  2. Create calculations with names (using `reaction::calc` and `setName`)

  - We'll set names for these variables, e.g., "varA" and "varB".
  - We'll create a calculation that depends on varA and varB, and set its name, e.g., "calculation".
  - We'll create an action that also depends on varA and varB, and set its name, e.g., "action".
  - Then we'll retrieve the names of each node (varA, varB, calculation, action) and combine them into a string.

 Note: The `reaction::action` is actually an alias for `reaction::calc` with a different trigger? 
        But according to the reference, `action` is defined as `calc<TR, IV, Fun, Args...>`. 
        So we can use it similarly to `calc`.

 However, note that the `action` function in the reference does not take a trigger policy or invalidation policy by default.
        It uses the same defaults as `calc` (ChangeTrig and KeepHandle). So we can use it as:

        auto myAction = reaction::action([&] { ... });

 Let's assume we are to use the default trigger and invalidation for the action.

 We'll combine the names in the order: varA, varB, calculation, action.

 Example return string: "varA,varB,calculation,action"

 But note: the problem says "combined names string", so we can use any separator. Let's use comma.

 Implementation:

  1. Create varA and varB with `reaction::var` and set their names.
  2. Create a calculation that uses varA and varB, and set its name.
  3. Create an action that uses varA and varB, and set its name.
  4. Use `getName()` on each to get the string and concatenate.

 However, note that the `reaction::var` and `reaction::calc` functions return a `React` object, which has the `setName` method.

 Let's write the code accordingly.

 Important: We must include the necessary headers. The given code already includes <reaction/reaction.h>.
            We are also in the namespace `test_reaction`.

 Let's implement the function.
output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Function implementations
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test naming reactive nodes and retrieving names
 *
 * @param valueA Initial value for varA
 * @param valueB Initial value for varB
 * @return std::string Combined string of retrieved names
 */
std::string test_named_reactions(int valueA, int valueB) {
    // Create reactive variables with names
    auto varA = reaction::var(valueA).setName("varA");
    auto varB = reaction::var(valueB).setName("varB");
    
    // Create calculation with name
    auto calculation = reaction::calc([&]() {
        return varA() + varB();
    }).setName("calculation");
    
    // Create action with name
    auto action = reaction::action([&]() {
        return varA() * varB();
    }).setName("action");
    
    // Retrieve names and combine into string
    std::string result = varA.getName() + "," + 
                        varB.getName() + "," + 
                        calculation.getName() + "," + 
                        action.getName();
    
    return result;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Helper types
// ============================================

class FieldOwnerNode : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_notificationCount++;
    }
    
    int getNotificationCount() const {
        return m_notificationCount;
    }

private:
    int m_notificationCount = 0;
};

class ProfileFields : public reaction::FieldBase {
public:
    ProfileFields(int initialScore, bool initialStatus)
        : m_score(initialScore), m_active(initialStatus) {}
    
    reaction::Var<int>& score() { return m_score; }
    reaction::Var<bool>& active() { return m_active; }

private:
    reaction::Var<int> m_score;
    reaction::Var<bool> m_active;
};

struct FieldBindingResult {
    int notificationsAfterScoreUpdate;
    int notificationsAfterStatusUpdate;
};

// ============================================
// Function implementation
// ============================================

FieldBindingResult test_field_binding(int initialScore, int scoreDelta) {
    // 1. Create ProfileFields object
    ProfileFields profileFields(initialScore, true);
    
    // 2. Create FieldOwnerNode
    FieldOwnerNode ownerNode;
    
    // 3. Get observer graph instance
    auto& observerGraph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // 4. Register owner node
    observerGraph.registerNode(&ownerNode);
    
    // 5. Get field graph instance
    auto& fieldGraph = reaction::FieldGraph::getInstance();
    
    // 6. Bind fields to owner node
    fieldGraph.bindField(profileFields.score().getId(), &ownerNode);
    fieldGraph.bindField(profileFields.active().getId(), &ownerNode);
    
    // 7. Update score field
    profileFields.score() += scoreDelta;
    
    // 8. Record notification count after score update
    int scoreCount = ownerNode.getNotificationCount();
    
    // 9. Update active field
    profileFields.active() = !profileFields.active();
    
    // 10. Record notification count after status update
    int statusCount = ownerNode.getNotificationCount();
    
    // 11. Return notification counts
    return FieldBindingResult{scoreCount, statusCount};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Helper types
// ============================================

class RecordingNode : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_notifications++;
    }
    
    int notificationCount() const {
        return m_notifications;
    }

private:
    int m_notifications = 0;
};

struct RollbackObservation {
    int notificationsAfterSecondBinding;
    int notificationsAfterRollback;
};

// ============================================
// Function implementation
// ============================================

RollbackObservation test_observer_state_rollback() {
    RollbackObservation result;
    
    // 1. Create RecordingNode instances
    auto node1 = std::make_shared<RecordingNode>();
    auto node2 = std::make_shared<RecordingNode>();
    
    // 2. Get observer graph instance
    auto& observerGraph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // 3. Register nodes
    observerGraph.registerNode(node1);
    observerGraph.registerNode(node2);
    
    // 4. Set up initial dependency: node1 observes node2
    observerGraph.addObserver(node1, node2);
    
    // 5. Save node state for rollback (save current observers of node2)
    auto originalObservers = observerGraph.getObservers(node2);
    
    // 6. Replace dependencies transactionally (remove all observers from node2)
    observerGraph.updateObserversTransactional(node2);
    
    // 7. Trigger notification on second sensor (node2) by changing node1's value
    // Since node2 no longer observes node1, this won't trigger any notifications
    // but we'll record the count anyway
    node1->valueChanged();
    result.notificationsAfterSecondBinding = node2->notificationCount();
    
    // 8. Record notification count after second binding (should be 0 since no observers)
    
    // 9. Execute rollback: restore original observers
    observerGraph.updateObserversTransactional(node2, originalObservers);
    
    // 10. Trigger notification on first sensor (node1)
    node1->valueChanged();
    
    // 11. Record notification count after rollback (should be 1 since node2 now observes node1 again)
    result.notificationsAfterRollback = node2->notificationCount();
    
    return result;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```

**Explanation:**

1. **Node Creation**: Creates two `RecordingNode` instances that override `valueChanged()` to track notifications.

2. **Graph Registration**: Uses `ObserverGraph::getInstance()` to get the singleton graph and registers both nodes.

3. **Initial Dependency**: Sets up node1 to observe node2 using `addObserver()`.

4. **State Saving**: Saves the current observer state of node2 before modification.

5. **Transactional Update**: Uses `updateObserversTransactional()` to clear all observers from node2.

6. **Notification Test**: Triggers node1 (first sensor) and records that node2 receives no notifications (0).

7. **Rollback**: Restores the original observer state using the saved observers.

8. **Final Test**: Triggers node1 again and confirms node2 receives notifications (1) after rollback.

The function returns a `RollbackObservation` struct containing the notification counts before and after the rollback operation.</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct BatchLifecycleResult {
    bool closedInitially;
    bool closedAfterClose;
    int recomputeCountAfterExecute;
};

BatchLifecycleResult test_batch_manual_lifecycle(int initialValue, int updatedValue) {
    // 1. Create reactive variable
    auto reactiveVar = reaction::var(initialValue);
    
    // 2. Create variable to track recomputation count
    int recomputeCount = 0;
    
    // 3. Create calculation that increments recompute count
    auto calculation = reaction::calc([&]() {
        recomputeCount++;
        return reactiveVar() * 2; // Simple calculation using reactive variable
    });
    
    // 4. Create Batch object
    reaction::Batch batch([&]() {
        reactiveVar.value(updatedValue); // Update reactive variable within batch
    });
    
    // 5. Check if batch is closed initially
    bool closedInitially = batch.isClosed();
    
    // 6. Execute batch
    batch.execute();
    
    // 7. Record recompute count after execute
    int recomputeCountAfterExecute = recomputeCount;
    
    // 8. Close batch
    batch.close();
    
    // 9. Check if batch is closed after close
    bool closedAfterClose = batch.isClosed();
    
    // 10. Ensure calculation is evaluated
    calculation(); // Force evaluation to ensure proper lifecycle
    
    // 11. Return batch lifecycle result
    return BatchLifecycleResult{
        closedInitially,
        closedAfterClose,
        recomputeCountAfterExecute
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```

This implementation follows all the specified requirements:

1. Creates a reactive variable using `reaction::var`
2. Initializes a counter to track recomputations
3. Creates a calculation using `reaction::calc` that depends on the reactive variable
4. Creates a Batch object with a lambda that updates the reactive variable
5. Checks the initial closed state using `isClosed()`
6. Executes the batch using `execute()`
7. Records the recompute count after execution
8. Closes the batch using `close()`
9. Verifies the closed state after closure
10. Ensures the calculation is evaluated
11. Returns the structured result with all required data points

The implementation correctly demonstrates the batch lifecycle management in the reaction framework.</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <array>
#include <cstddef>
#include <reaction/memory/sbo_resource.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct SmallPayload {
    int value;
    bool operator!=(const SmallPayload& other) const = default;
};

struct LargePayload {
    std::array<int, 64> data{};
    bool operator!=(const LargePayload& other) const = default;
};

struct SboStorageStatus {
    bool updateReportedChange;
    bool smallUsingSBO;
    std::size_t smallStackUsage;
    bool largeInitialized;
    bool largeUsingSBO;
};

SboStorageStatus test_sbo_storage_info(int initialSmall,
                                       int updatedSmall,
                                       int largeSeed) {
    // 1. Create lambda function to make large payload
    auto createLargePayload = [seed = largeSeed]() {
        LargePayload payload;
        std::fill(payload.data.begin(), payload.data.end(), seed);
        return payload;
    };
    
    // 2. Create small SBOResource
    reaction::memory::SBOResource<SmallPayload> smallResource;
    smallResource.updateValue(SmallPayload{initialSmall});
    
    // 3. Create large SBOResource
    reaction::memory::SBOResource<LargePayload> largeResource;
    largeResource.updateValue(createLargePayload());
    
    // 4. Update small resource value
    bool reportedChange = smallResource.updateValue(SmallPayload{updatedSmall});
    
    // 5. Get storage information for small resource
    auto smallInfo = smallResource.getStorageInfo();
    
    // 6. Get storage information for large resource
    auto largeInfo = largeResource.getStorageInfo();
    
    // 7. Return SBO storage status
    return SboStorageStatus{
        .updateReportedChange = reportedChange,
        .smallUsingSBO = smallInfo.using_sbo,
        .smallStackUsage = smallInfo.stack_usage,
        .largeInitialized = largeInfo.initialized,
        .largeUsingSBO = largeInfo.using_sbo
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct PointerAccessSnapshot {
    int valueAfterFirstAdd;
    int valueAfterSecondAdd;
    bool pointerStable;
};

/**
 * Function: Demonstrate pointer-style access for reactive structs.
 *
 * @param initialValue Initial value stored inside the Counter
 * @param delta Amount added on each pointer invocation
 * @return PointerAccessSnapshot Snapshot capturing results after pointer operations
 */
inline PointerAccessSnapshot test_react_pointer_access(int initialValue, int delta) {
    // 1. Define Counter struct with add method
    struct Counter {
        int value;
        
        Counter(int val) : value(val) {}
        
        void add(int increment) {
            value += increment;
        }
        
        int get() const { return value; }
    };
    
    // 2. Create reactive variable with Counter
    auto counterVar = reaction::var(Counter(initialValue));
    
    // 3. Get first pointer
    auto firstPtr = counterVar.getPtr();
    
    // 4. Call add method through pointer
    firstPtr->add(delta);
    
    // 5. Get current value after first add
    int valueAfterFirstAdd = counterVar.get().get();
    
    // 6. Get second pointer
    auto secondPtr = counterVar.getPtr();
    
    // 7. Call add method through pointer again
    secondPtr->add(delta);
    
    // 8. Get current value after second add
    int valueAfterSecondAdd = counterVar.get().get();
    
    // 9. Check if pointer is stable
    bool pointerStable = (firstPtr.get() == secondPtr.get());
    
    // 10. Return pointer access snapshot
    return PointerAccessSnapshot{
        valueAfterFirstAdd,
        valueAfterSecondAdd,
        pointerStable
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

namespace test_reaction {

struct ValiditySnapshot {

    return snapshot;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction

#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>

namespace test_reaction {

class CountingObserver : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_notifications++;
    }
    int notifications() const {
        return m_notifications;
    }

private:
    int m_notifications = 0;
};

struct ObserverNodeLinkSnapshot {
    int firstNotificationsAfterInitial;
    int firstNotificationsAfterUpdate;
    int secondNotificationsAfterUpdate;
    int thirdNotificationsAfterUpdate;
};

ObserverNodeLinkSnapshot test_observer_node_links() {
    // 1. Create CountingObserver instance
    auto observer = std::make_shared<CountingObserver>();

    // 2. Create three source ObserverNode instances
    auto source1 = std::make_shared<reaction::ObserverNode>();
    auto source2 = std::make_shared<reaction::ObserverNode>();
    auto source3 = std::make_shared<reaction::ObserverNode>();

    // 3. Get observer graph instance
    auto& graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();

    // 4. Register all nodes
    graph.addNode(observer);
    graph.addNode(source1);
    graph.addNode(source2);
    graph.addNode(source3);

    // 5. Attach single observer (CountingObserver) to first source
    source1->addOneObserver(observer);

    // 6. Trigger notification on first source
    source1->valueChanged();

    // 7. Get notification count after initial setup
    int firstNotificationsAfterInitial = observer->notifications();

    // 8. Rewire dependencies: detach from first and attach to second
    source1->removeOneObserver(observer);
    source2->addOneObserver(observer);

    // 9. Trigger notification on old source (first source) and get count
    source1->valueChanged();
    int firstNotificationsAfterUpdate = observer->notifications();

    // 10. Trigger notification on second source and get count
    source2->valueChanged();
    int secondNotificationsAfterUpdate = observer->notifications();

    // 11. Trigger notification on third source and get count
    source3->valueChanged();
    int thirdNotificationsAfterUpdate = observer->notifications();

    // 12. Close all nodes
    observer->close();
    source1->close();
    source2->close();
    source3->close();

    // 13. Return observer node link snapshot
    return ObserverNodeLinkSnapshot{
        firstNotificationsAfterInitial,
        firstNotificationsAfterUpdate,
        secondNotificationsAfterUpdate,
        thirdNotificationsAfterUpdate
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```

      #pragma once

      #include <reaction/cache/cache_base.h>
      #include <memory>
      #include <chrono>

      namespace test_reaction {

      struct GenericCacheStatsSnapshot {
          std::size_t entriesBefore;
          std::size_t entriesAfterInsert;
          std::size_t entriesAfterInvalidate;
          std::size_t hitCount;
          std::size_t missCount;
          double hitRatio;
          bool firstLookupWasMiss;
          bool secondLookupWasHit;
      };

      GenericCacheStatsSnapshot test_generic_cache_base() {
          // Define IntCache class
          class IntCache : public reaction::CacheBase<int, int> {
          public:
              using reaction::CacheBase<int, int>::getStats;
              using reaction::CacheBase<int, int>::getCachedValue;
              using reaction::CacheBase<int, int>::cacheValue;
              using reaction::CacheBase<int, int>::invalidateAllEntries;
              using reaction::CacheBase<int, int>::triggerCleanup;
          };

          // Create an instance of IntCache
          IntCache cache;

          // Get initial cache statistics
          auto initialStats = cache.getStats();

          // Perform initial lookup for key 1
          auto firstLookup = cache.getCachedValue(1);

          // Insert value 100 for key 1
          cache.cacheValue(1, 100);

          // Perform second lookup for key 1
          auto secondLookup = cache.getCachedValue(1);

          // Get cache statistics after insertion
          auto afterInsertStats = cache.getStats();

          // Invalidate all cache entries
          cache.invalidateAllEntries();

          // Trigger cleanup
          cache.triggerCleanup();

          // Get cache statistics after invalidation
          auto afterInvalidationStats = cache.getStats();

          // Prepare the snapshot
          GenericCacheStatsSnapshot snapshot;
          snapshot.entriesBefore = initialStats.totalEntries;
          snapshot.entriesAfterInsert = afterInsertStats.totalEntries;
          snapshot.entriesAfterInvalidate = afterInvalidationStats.totalEntries;
          snapshot.hitCount = afterInvalidationStats.hitCount;
          snapshot.missCount = afterInvalidationStats.missCount;
          snapshot.hitRatio = static_cast<double>(snapshot.hitCount) / (snapshot.hitCount + snapshot.missCount);
          snapshot.firstLookupWasMiss = (firstLookup == nullptr);
          snapshot.secondLookupWasHit = (secondLookup != nullptr);

          return snapshot;
      }

      }  // namespace test_reaction

#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <reaction/expression/expression_builders.h>

namespace test_reaction {

/**
 * Struct: RawExpressionSnapshot
 *
 * Captures the values produced by raw expression builder helpers
 * (`make_binary_expr`, `make_unary_expr`) before and after updating
 * their reactive inputs.
 */
struct RawExpressionSnapshot {
    int initialSum;
    int initialNegatedSum;
    int initialChainedValue;

    int updatedSum;
    int updatedNegatedSum;
    int updatedChainedValue;
};

/**
 * Function: test_raw_expression_builders
 *
 * Demonstrates how low-level expression builder helpers
 * (`make_binary_expr`, `make_unary_expr`) can be combined with
 * `expr` to build reactive calculations without using the higher
 * level `make_binary_op` / `make_unary_op` shortcuts.
 *
 * The function:
 * - Creates three reactive integers A, B, C.
 * - Builds sum = A + B via make_binary_expr.
 * - Builds negated = -(A + B) via make_unary_expr(make_binary_expr(...)).
 * - Builds chained = -(A + B) * C via nested make_* calls.
 * - Updates A and C and records all expression values before and after.
 *
 * @param a Initial value for the first input
 * @param b Initial value for the second input
 * @param c Initial value for the chaining multiplier
 * @return RawExpressionSnapshot
 *         Snapshot of sum / negated / chained values before and after updates.
 */
inline RawExpressionSnapshot test_raw_expression_builders(int a, int b, int c);

}  // namespace test_reaction
```

#pragma once

#include <reaction/expression/atomic_operations.h>
#include <cstdint>

namespace test_reaction {

/**
 * Struct: AtomicHelperSnapshot
 *
 * Records the value of an integer after a sequence of atomic compound helper
 * operations and whether each operation reported a change.
 */
struct AtomicHelperSnapshot {
    int32_t finalValue;
    bool addReportedChange;
    bool multiplyReportedChange;
    bool andReportedChange;
    bool leftShiftReportedChange;
    bool incrementReportedChange;
};

/**
 * Helper implementation type that mimics the minimal interface required by
 * reaction::atomicCompoundAssign helpers. It stores an integer and exposes
 * an atomicOperation method that receives a lambda and tracks whether the
 * value was changed.
 */
class IntAtomicImpl {
public:
    explicit IntAtomicImpl(int32_t initial) : m_value(initial), m_lastReportedChange(false) {}

    template <typename F>
    void atomicOperation(F&& op) {
        bool changed = op(m_value);
        m_lastReportedChange = changed;
    }

    template <typename F>
    void atomicOperation(F&& op, bool forceNotify) {
        bool changed = op(m_value);
        m_lastReportedChange = forceNotify ? true : changed;
    }

    int32_t value() const { return m_value; }
    bool lastReportedChange() const { return m_lastReportedChange; }

private:
    int32_t m_value;
    bool m_lastReportedChange;
};

/**
 * Function: test_atomic_compound_helpers
 *
 * Demonstrates the low-level atomic compound helper APIs:
 * - atomicCompoundAssign
 * - atomicAddAssign
 * - atomicMultiplyAssign
 * - atomicBitwiseAndAssign
 * - atomicLeftShiftAssign
 * - atomicIncrement
 *
 * These helpers operate on a simple IntAtomicImpl type instead of a full
 * React wrapper, making it easy to observe value and change reporting.
 *
 * @param initial Initial integer value
 * @return AtomicHelperSnapshot
 *         Snapshot containing the final value and which operations reported
 *         that a change occurred.
 */
inline AtomicHelperSnapshot test_atomic_compound_helpers(int32_t initial) {
    IntAtomicImpl impl(initial);
    
    // Test atomicAddAssign
    reaction::atomicAddAssign(impl, 5);
    bool addChanged = impl.lastReportedChange();
    
    // Test atomicMultiplyAssign
    reaction::atomicMultiplyAssign(impl, 3);
    bool multiplyChanged = impl.lastReportedChange();
    
    // Test atomicBitwiseAndAssign
    reaction::atomicBitwiseAndAssign(impl, 0x0F);
    bool andChanged = impl.lastReportedChange();
    
    // Test atomicLeftShiftAssign
    reaction::atomicLeftShiftAssign(impl, 2);
    bool leftShiftChanged = impl.lastReportedChange();
    
    // Test atomicIncrement
    reaction::atomicIncrement(impl);
    bool incrementChanged = impl.lastReportedChange();
    
    return AtomicHelperSnapshot{
        impl.value(),
        addChanged,
        multiplyChanged,
        andChanged,
        leftShiftChanged,
        incrementChanged
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>
